raw_consumer_statement:
Hi, I would like to complain about persistent fraud on my AMEX credit card as below : Timeline XX/XX/year> - 1 fraud transactions merchant XXXX XX/XX/year> - 12 fraud transactions merchant XXXX XX/XX/year> - see below The first 13 fraudulent transactions ( XXXX & XXXX ) were identified and communicated on XXXX XX/XX/year> over the phone with both Customer Services and Fraud Team on individual calls ( same day, same call ).

The AMEX card ending XXXX was deactivated immediately and XXXX merchant blocked from all AMEX card transactivity according to customer services/fraud team based on individual conversations had with each.

( Tone of conversation had with Customer Services/Fraud Team : `` I have no idea what XXXX is, they have charged me in non-local currency XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX , I have no understanding or need for this merchant I have not been abroad to any location relating to any of the countries in question, block all activities with this merchant immediately please '' ).

However, despite issuing a new AMEX card, deactivating the old AMEX card and merchant hard block on XXXX we have 4 new instances of fraudulent transactions subsequent to the identification and remedies undertaken on 4th XX/XX/year> : XXXX XX/XX/year> - 4 fraud transactions merchant XXXX  Rather more concerningly, these newly occurring fraudulent transactions took place on the newly issued credit card ending XXXX ( i.e. This AMEX card was newly issued to prevent further instances of fraud going forwards from XXXX XX/XX/year> onwards ). 

Today, XXXX XX/XX/year>, I have been advised by Customer Services/Fraud Team that the merchant block on XXXX never took place as it should have done the previous month, and when they tried to block the merchant today ( like they claimed to have done on XXXX XX/XX/year> ), they were unable to because the fraud team had deactivated the AMEX card ending XXXX earlier the same day in the call prior ; as the customer services team could not authenticate the AMEX card security number ( due to a system level security restriction imposed by the Fraud Team ) .

This is really shockingly seriously poor customer services and shockingly bad systems, controls and governance processes.

I am appalled that this fraudulent activity would persist and then to have it repeat on a new AMEX card that had been issued since this was the identified resolution to prevent.

But for Customer Services and Fraud Teams to not undertake their individual duties to protect customers is obviously the worst issue in my mind, in this case by neglecting to block the merchant as agreed. 

Regards, XXXX XXXX XXXX
